title: N-Channel MOSFET as a Low-Side Power Switch
content: An N-channel MOSFET switches a load on and off from a logic-level MCU signal by controlling V_GS.  When V_GS > V_th the MOSFET enters the triode (on) region; when V_GS < V_th it is off.
extended_content: Low-side switch configuration: drain connected to load, source to GND; gate driven from MCU GPIO via series resistor (100 Ω – 10 kΩ). Selection criteria: V_DS(max) > supply + transient spikes, I_D(max) > worst-case load current, R_DS(on) low to minimise conduction loss (P = I²·R_DS(on)).  Gate capacitance C_iss sets switching speed; fast switching (< 1 µs) reduces transition losses.  Inductive loads (solenoids, motors) require a flyback diode (D from drain to supply rail) to suppress V_DS spikes when the MOSFET turns off.  Typical devices: IRLZ44N (logic-level, 55 V, 47 A), AO3400 (20 V, 5.7 A, SOT-23).
source: Sedra & Smith, Microelectronic Circuits, 7th ed.; Texas Instruments AN-7836
